title: qPCR for Differentiation of Halal-Critical Animal Species in Food Products
author: Sophian, Alfi
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Halal   food     authentication     is     essential     for     ensuring     consumer     trust  ,   religious     compliance  ,   and   global   trade     integrity  .   Adulteration     or     cross-contamination     of   non-halal   species     such   as   porcine     derivatives     remains     a     persistent     challenge   in   the     food     industry  . Real-  time   PCR (  qPCR  )   provides     a     sensitive  ,   specific  ,   and     rapid     solution     for     differentiating     animal     species     at     the   DNA level,   even   in   highly     processed     products  . By   enabling     precise     species     identification  ,   qPCR     supports   halal   certification     processes     and     prevents     fraudulent     labeling  .   This     policy     brief     highlights     the     need     for     national     strategies     to     implement     qPCR-based     authentication   in   food     safety     programs  .   Strengthening     laboratory     networks  ,   developing     standardized   testing   protocols  ,   and     training     personnel   are   crucial     steps     toward     a     robust   halal   assurance     system     that     safeguards     consumer     confidence     and     supports     international     market     access  .
